content: Hierarchical planner modules for task decomposition can leverage the naturally emergent episodic memory properties of Transformer attention heads to build a dynamic, as-needed decomposition system that only splits tasks into subtasks when attention patterns signal a mismatch between the current horizon and existing episodic task memories. This design replaces fixed hierarchical decomposition structures with attention-driven, human-like memory retrieval that prioritizes early and recent completed task schemas just like human episodic memory.
source_topics: Planner module design for task decomposition
Transformer attention mechanism for episodic memory
trigger_topic: Planner module design for task decomposition
